items.title: Secure area with one exit left hospital staff trapped by armed patient

children.content: No-one was injured during the incident at Canberra Hospital but staff were stuck inside a "secure staff station" for three hours while police disarmed a man with a knife.

image.altText: A sign that says "Emergency Building 5'' and "Main entry'' pointing to a building.

items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

image.altText: A man stands and smiles, leaning on a bench in a large workshop space with buckets and gear behind him.

items.title: 'Self-belief, respect': Indigenous boxers building strength with David Pocock

children.content: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

gallery.altText: A young boy hits a punching bag in a boxing gym.

thumbnail.altText: A young boy hits a punching bag in a boxing gym.

items.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

image.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

items.title: 'Not what the community wanted': Big Splash lease spared despite backlash

children.content: ACT planning authorities have decided they will not follow through with a threat to terminate the lease of Big Splash water park, saying they are now satisfied the operators have made moves to reopen later this year.

image.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Want to buy a football team? Why this club's next chapter is unwritten

children.content: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

items.title: A simple message of love: Memorial held at Port Arthur 30 years on from the massacre

children.content: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

children.content: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa is spared jail after a court found her novel fetishised young children.

image.altText: Lauren Mastrosa outside court wearing sunglasses and black jacket.

items.title: 'Almost bullying': Private hospitals blame closures on 'cowboy' insurers

children.content: A former Bupa executive accuses health insurers of using "almost bullying" tactics in their negotiations with private hospitals, while the Australian Private Hospitals Association says insurers have become "cowboys" in need of regulation.

items.title: Jimmy Kimmel says joke misconstrued as Trumps call to fire late-night host

items.title: Coalition points to stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stop-gap

children.content: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

items.title: Man who randomly killed British tourist not criminally responsible

children.content: A man who killed a British tourist in a random stabbing at an Albury motel in 2024 has been found not criminally responsible for the attack.

image.altText: Police car parked out the front of a motel.

items.title: Bus driver had 'microsleep' before fatal crash, jury hears

children.content: The first police officers to arrive on the scene of a fatal bus crash in north Queensland have told the court of their conversations with the driver in the immediate aftermath of the incident.

items.title: The soy sauce fish is a perfect design — except on one important point

items.title: How to live on other mobs' Country, respectfully

children.content: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

children.content: Bunbury fisherman Mason Jones, who led a protest against the WA government's demersal fishing ban, says he has no intention of stopping the campaign.

items.title: Perth man pleads not guilty to father's murder on grounds of insanity

children.content: David Moussa, 32, pleads not guilty on grounds of insanity to murdering his father in the Perth suburb of Clarkson.

items.title: Queensland waste company to fight allegations of nuisance odours

items.title: Affidavit reveals more details as man charged with attempted Trump assassination

items.title: Commuters warned rail disruptions could worsen ahead of Magic Round

items.title: NSW public transport Opal network is set to change. Here's what we know

children.content: Police are investigating whether recent arson attacks across 15 Melbourne hospitality venues are linked to an organised crime syndicate.

items.title: Nedra Talley Ross of the Ronettes, who sang Baby I Love You, dies

children.content: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

image.altText: A woman in a gold evening dress, holds a prize, which is a figurine made of highly polished metal.

items.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago

children.content: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.

items.title: 'System … is broken': Mounjaro won't be listed on PBS in 'stand-off' with US maker

children.content: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

items.title: How a $2.2 trillion dream city in the Saudi desert got wound back

children.content: Saudi Arabia's plans to transform its economy were already facing headwinds when the Iran war broke out, placing them even further in jeopardy.

items.title: NRL's greatest ever try-scorer hints at imminent PNG switch
